Q5 Not checked
General Aptitude • INSTRUMENTATION ENGINEERING • Beginner • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

How many three digit numbers can be generated from 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9 such that the digits are in ascending order?

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
If the first digit of the three digit number is 1 and the second digit is 2, then the last digit can be
filled in 7 ways (either by 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8 and 9).
Similarly, if second digit is 3, then the last digit can be filled in 6 ways and so on.
The same method is applicable for digit 2, 3, _ _ _ _ 7 at the first place. Hence, the total three digit numbers with digits in ascending order,
= (7 + 6 + 5 + 4 + 3 + 2 + 1) + (6 + 5 + 4 + 3 + 2 + 1) + _ _ __ _ _ _ _ + (2 + 1) + 1
= 28 + 21 + 15 + 10 + 6 + 3 + 1 = 84
